trade.summary: Bilateral trade has collapsed since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Norway imposed comprehensive sanctions aligned with EU and G7 measures. Cross-border traffic at the Storskog checkpoint (Norway's only land border crossing with Russia) has been severely restricted. Historic trade in fish, seafood, and Arctic goods is largely suspended. Some limited humanitarian and practical border arrangements remain.
disputes: Norway banned Russian fishing vessels from Norwegian ports
Disputes over Svalbard Russian presence and coal mining rights

diplomatic.status: severely strained — ambassadors maintained but relations frozen
diplomatic.summary: Norway-Russia relations have deteriorated sharply since the 2022 Ukraine invasion. Norway has joined all EU/NATO sanctions packages, expelled Russian intelligence officers, and restricted the Russian diplomatic presence. The two countries share a 196 km Arctic border and historically cooperated on fisheries (Barents Sea joint management) and Arctic governance — both suspended or frozen. Norway has designated Russia a primary security threat in its defense reviews.
recent_incidents: Russia accused Norway of violating Svalbard Treaty obligations
Norway expelled Russian intelligence officers posing as diplomats (2022-2024)
Russian military aircraft provocations near Norwegian airspace

military.summary: Norway, as a founding NATO member with a direct Arctic border with Russia, is at the center of NATO's northern flank posture. Norway has dramatically increased defense spending, acquired F-35s, and expanded Norwegian Armed Forces in the north. NATO exercises in Norway have intensified. Russia has increased Kola Peninsula military activity and conducted provocative flights near Norwegian airspace. The historic Norway-Russia military transparency and incident-prevention frameworks have broken down.
military.alliance_status: adversarial — Norway is NATO, Russia is non-member adversary
incidents: Multiple Russian military aircraft intercepted near Norwegian airspace
Russian submarine activity in Norwegian Sea increased
Russian military buildup on Kola Peninsula monitored by Norwegian intelligence

financial.summary: Norwegian sovereign wealth fund divested all Russian holdings following the 2022 invasion. Norwegian companies have exited Russian operations. Financial sanctions prohibit most transactions. Historically minimal financial integration beyond energy sector; now essentially zero.

energy.summary: No bilateral energy trade. Historically, both countries were Arctic energy producers with some coordination in Barents Sea development (e.g., Shtokman gas field discussions). All such cooperation is suspended. Norway has actually benefited economically from replacing Russian gas in European markets.

scientific.summary: Arctic scientific cooperation, once a rare area of productive engagement (Joint Norwegian-Russian Barents Sea ecosystem research, environmental monitoring), has been largely suspended. Norwegian research institutions have ended formal partnerships with Russian counterparts. Some informal data sharing on fisheries management continues due to the practical necessity of managing shared fish stocks.
